confidence_rationale: Video is a research presentation slide deck with title slide, study background, methods, results graphs, and conclusions. No actual performance footage of peg transfer task is visible. This is educational/instructional material describing a randomized controlled trial comparing reversed camera view practice versus orientation drills for laparoscopic peg transfer in novice surgeons.
cannot_determine: Completion time — no performance video present
Phase timings — no task phases executed in this content
FLS score — not scorable as no performance demonstration
technique_summary: Not applicable. This is a research presentation, not a performance video.

demonstrated_techniques: Baseline FLS peg transfer with standard camera orientation (forward view)
Peg transfer with reversed camera orientation (180 degrees)
Spatial orientation drills using instrument tracing with reversed camera view
verbal_cues: Surgical residents take 470% longer to complete reversed camera peg transfer tasks
Experienced surgeons take 65% longer with reversed camera orientation
Greatest performance improvement occurs after 11 repetitions of reversed camera peg transfer practice
common_errors_addressed: Disorientation in reversed camera view — addressed by dedicated spatial orientation drills
Inefficient transfer technique in reversed orientation — improved significantly with task-specific reversed camera practice (p < 0.01)
teaching_content.study_findings: Practicing a validated task (peg transfer) with reversed camera view confers significant performance improvement, with greatest change after 11 repetitions. Spatial orientation drills with reversed camera do not confer significant improvement compared to forward view practice alone.

scoring_note: This content is a research presentation and does not contain scorable performance footage. No FLS task score is generated. Content is classified as instructional and used only for extraction of teaching/learning points.
